summaryForNext: Two seeded goals split on inspection. GW1 (mcp.tool.call) is fully implementable with NO new port — McpClient::call_tool + its JSON-RPC adapter are real end-to-end; only a catalog entry + a dispatch_mcp runner split (the Mcp arm is currently flat list_tools) + schema + tests are missing. GW2 (real application.deploy write) is BLOCKED and must NOT be faked: GateAdmin has only list_routes, the adapter only does GET /routes, and no gate admin WRITE contract is verified — so application.deploy stays a declared refused write with the dependency documented (it already refuses cleanly, it is not a {}-stub). GW3/GW4 harden around GW1 (clean unknown-mcp-kind refusal + a not-logged-arguments test). Recommendation: 2 changes (1 impl: mcp.tool.call; 1 docs/honest-refusal: application.deploy reframe). mcp.tool.call role = operator floor. Analyze can --skip (no external research; agent-owned wiring against existing ports).
openQuestions: Q1 mcp.tool.call role operator vs admin — defaulting operator (recommended)
Q2 single vs multi downstream MCP server in input — single-endpoint this phase (adapter is single-endpoint; YAGNI)
Q3 tighten GW2 refusal message to name missing gate write contract — recommended yes, cheap
